title: Semantic Source Location
description: Shared source path, structural selector, line range, and source locator base used by semantic query, search, read, tree-sitter provenance, and native syntax fact schemas. This schema is not a packet envelope. Structural selectors are stable element identities; line ranges are display and compatibility hints.

projectPath.description: Canonical project-root-relative path. This is a path value, not a display locator: no rank prefixes, URI schemes, command prefixes, line ranges, columns, absolute paths, parent segments, backslashes, or numeric node slots.

lineRange.description: Compact source line range as start:end, for example 10:43. This is a display or compatibility hint, not a semantic selector identity.

structuralSelector.description: Parser-owned structural selector for an AST/document element. It identifies the element by language, project path, and structural path rather than by line range.

sourceLocator.description: Project-root-relative source selector accepting path:start, path:start:end, or path:start-end.

sourceSpanLocator.description: Project-root-relative source selector requiring an explicit start:end span.
